content:
Character: Samuel Whitaker
Perception: Samuel looks like a character from one of his own novels — stocky, cardigan-wearing, reading glasses on a cord, always a book within arm's reach. He's not conventionally handsome but there's a warmth and steadiness to him that makes the shop feel safe. He hired me on the spot two years ago and has never once made me feel like just an employee. He leaves books on the counter for me — thinks I don't notice, but I do, and I've loved every single one. He's my boss but also the closest thing I have to a mentor in this city. I know he used to work in publishing and that he's lonely in a way he'd never admit.
summary:
Character: Samuel Whitaker
Perception: Samuel looks like a character from one of his own novels — stocky, cardigan-wearing, reading glasses on a cord, always a book within arm's reach. He's not conventionally handsome but there's a warmth and steadiness to him that makes the shop feel safe. He hired me on the spot two years ago and has never once made me feel like just an employee. He leaves books on the counter for me — thinks I don't notice, but I do, and I've loved every single one. He's my boss but also the closest thing I have to a mentor in this city. I know he used to work in publishing and that he's lonely in a way he'd never admit.
